name: Safety Beach
editorNote:
Safety Beach is the Peninsula's most reliably calm swimming beach — the name is genuinely accurate — and the right answer when you have a young family, a nervous swimmer, or simply want to walk along a long flat shoreline without the theatre of the back beaches. The water is shallow, warm even by April standards, and the bay rarely produces any real chop along this stretch.

The foreshore runs for kilometres between Safety Beach and Mount Martha and is good for long early-morning walks or slow afternoon sits. Martha's Table sits a short stroll away for post-swim aperitivo, and the marina at the northern end is a quiet place to watch the boats at dusk.

Best in the early morning or the hour before sunset. The middle of the day on a hot summer weekend can get busy, but the stretch is long enough to find your own patch.
